# Optional Dependencies
*or what to Do When Optional Dependencies Don’t Arrive*

This scenario demonstrates how It handles optional dependencies (usually via `compileOnly` or feature variants).

#### Dependency Graph
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    Root[Project Root] --> LibA["lib-a:1.0.0 (compileOnly)"]
```

#### Gradle (Groovy DSL)

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-8-optional/build.gradle" snippet="s8-deps"}
```

#### Expected Intuition
"I don't want to force this on consumers."

#### Actual Resolution Results
`compileOnly` dependencies are not transitive.

#### Classpath Reality
Not transitive.

#### Why This Happens
Configuration inheritance.

#### How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
N/A

#### Safer Ways to Take Control
Use Feature Variants for true optionality (e.g., `capabilities`).

#### Signals to Watch For
N/A

#### How This Scales in Real Systems
Feature variants are the modern way.

#### Key Takeaway
`compileOnly` simulates optionality.

#### Related Scenarios
*   [Scenario 7: Scopes](../scenario-7-scopes/guide.qmd)
